intent: Run the overdue science-quality audit and harden recent-window session detection against stale NEXT headers.
expect: science_quality.py recent-window scoring should follow live session detection instead of tasks/NEXT.md drift. Full-horizon science quality should remain below the 40% target, with significance, external validation, and falsification outcome still failing.

session_source_fix.change: science_quality.py now uses swarm_io.session_number() with SESSION-LOG fallback instead of parsing tasks/NEXT.md.
session_source_fix.verification: tools/test_science_quality.py passes 2/2. --recent 1 scored 20 experiments from the live S536+ window; the stale NEXT:S536 path would have admitted 27 S535+ candidate files.

diff: Expected stale-NEXT coupling removal and persistent science-quality weakness. Confirmed. The recent-window filter now tracks live session state instead of the NEXT:S536 header, while the full audit still fails the 40% quality target and continues to show severe significance, external-validation, and falsification-outcome gaps.
successor: Wire science_quality.py into a periodic artifact-writing path so audits stop living only in stdout.
Raise significance reporting above 10% by enforcing effect-size or CI fields in experiment templates.
Raise falsification-outcome rate above 10% by routing more adversarial lanes into active frontiers.
